compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: STATUS 2026-07-12 (/rebar-implement): CLAIMED (in_progress), plan HARDENED + signed through the claim gate, NOT yet implemented. New G7 leaf-parent-containment plan-review criterion. KEY design (settled during claim remediation): G7 is exec:AGENT and reads the parent ticket at RUNTIME via its always-available show_ticket tool (parent_id is already in the ticket-graph context via pass1._ticket_graph_blob) — NO new context-plumbing. Register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER but NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED (tooling is granted by agentic execution_mode per registry.py:62-65, not by CODEBASE_GROUNDED; G7 grounds against the ticket store, not code). Add a require_parent_id applies_at axis, validated in criteria/overlay.py::_validate_routing_entry (190-212) AND registry.validate_packaged_routing, threaded via orchestrator.route_criteria. New reviewers/plan_review_G7.md rubric (parent-wins; instructs show_ticket(parent_id)) + one parent-realign coach move in passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY + add G7 to workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml criteria list + regenerate docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md. Ships advisory 0.95; promotion-path doc committed at docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md (fire-rate jq query over plan_review_result_v1 sidecars + 0.6-0.7 band). REMAINING: implement under held-out TDD (routing fires leaf-with-parent only; fixture parent/leaf semantics contained/exceeding/contradicting; validate_packaged_routing()==[]), commit, push, close.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
A significant epic failure showed leaf plans DRIFT as leaf tickets are claimed and edited: by epic close, the leaf ACs described a very different plan than what the epic said would be delivered, and nothing caught the divergence until the epic's completion verification. No current criterion checks a leaf plan against its PARENT'S plan — G1G2/E4 ground against the codebase, COH checks internal coherence. IMPORTANT CORRECTION (verified against the code): there is NO existing "ancestor carve-out" that injects a parent's PLAN TEXT — `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281) injects only the parent's ticket ID string into context. So the leaf-vs-parent comparison is NEW behavior this ticket adds — implemented by having G7's AGENT read the parent via its show_ticket tool at runtime (no new context-plumbing), not by mirroring a (non-existent) injection carve-out. The review that gates the CLAIM is the right moment to catch drift, ticket by ticket, instead of at epic close.

## What
- Add a new plan-review criterion (working id `G7`, facet parent-containment) to src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json + the registry: `exec: AGENT` (it must read the parent ticket from the store, like the other codebase-grounded criteria), with a rubric prompt at src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ following the existing G-series prompt conventions.
- ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets WITH a parent: the gate is (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children). MECHANISM (the existing schema can't express "has_parent_id" — `applies()` in registry.py:311-339 gates only on `scope` (container/leaf), `suppress_types`, `suppress_when`): add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id` to the `applies_at` schema and to `applies()`, and thread the ticket's `has_parent_id` fact through `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py:337-375, which calls `registry.applies()`) from the assembled PlanContext (which already knows the parent chain). So G7's routing = `applies_at: {scope: ["leaf"], require_parent_id: true, suppress_types: ["bug","session_log"]}`. The `scope:["leaf"]` gives "no children"; `require_parent_id` gives "has a parent"; bugs/session_logs stay exempt as today; parentless tickets and containers never run it. The `require_parent_id` axis: `_validate_routing_entry` (overlay.py:190-212) currently does NOT reject unknown applies_at keys (so the key would pass unvalidated) — this ticket ADDS an explicit `require_parent_id` boolean-type check there (non-bool raises), and `registry.validate_packaged_routing()` stays green on the packaged G7 entry.
- SEMANTICS: the leaf's What/Scope/ACs must be a SUBSET of the parent's declared scope — a finding fires when the leaf (a) delivers something the parent's plan does not contain or implies is out of scope, (b) contradicts a parent AC/success criterion, or (c) redefines a deliverable the parent specifies differently. Consistent narrowing (a leaf doing PART of the parent) is NOT a finding.
- CONFLICT RULE (the operator's directive, encoded in the rubric AND the Pass-4 coaching move): the PARENT WINS — it holds the full delivery context. Coaching directs the agent to realign the leaf to the parent; if the agent believes the parent is genuinely wrong, the productive move is to update the PARENT first (which stales the parent's own attestation and forces its re-review) and only then re-review the leaf — never to silently diverge the leaf.
- CONTEXT ASSEMBLY — NO new context plumbing (the ticket's original intent, verified against the code): G7 is `exec: AGENT`, and the pydantic_ai runner provides the read-only `show_ticket` tool to EVERY AGENT criterion (src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py — "`show_ticket` always"; the completion verifier reads tickets the same way, completion.py:18). So G7's agent FETCHES the parent at runtime: its rubric instructs it to call `show_ticket(<parent_id>)` (and optionally the grandparent) and compare the leaf's scope against the parent's plan text. The parent_id is ALREADY in the agent's context: `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281) emits a `parent: <parent_id>` line from `ctx.state['parent_id']` (pass1.py:288). So G7's agent reads that parent_id from its ticket-graph context and calls `show_ticket(parent_id)` ONCE for the parent's plan text (and optionally the grandparent) — one hop, via the always-available `show_ticket` tool, nothing else pre-injected. This means NO `parent_plan` field on PlanContext, NO `_assemble_context_uncached` change, NO `plan_review_verify_inputs`/verifier-prompt slot, NO pass1 finder-context change — the agent reads the parent on demand through its existing tool. To bound cost, the rubric directs the agent to read only the parent (and one grandparent), not the whole subtree.
- Pass-2/3 stay untouched: findings flow through the standard verify -> decide pipeline; severity maps onto the existing plan axes (divergent_implementation is the natural home — a leaf diverging from its parent is exactly a divergent-implementation signal).
- POSTURE: ship `default_posture: advisory` at 0.95 per the ADR-0036 expand-contract house pattern, BUT record fire-rate + adjudication from day one. IN-SESSION deliverable (the promotion-decision PATH, not the decision itself), as a COMMITTED REPO ARTIFACT (CI-verifiable that the file exists, not a one-time ticket comment): a new committed file `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` documenting (a) the exact query — a **jq** query over the `plan_review_result_v1` REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (`.schema == "plan_review_result_v1"`), computing G7 fire-rate = (count of sidecars whose `.findings[]` has "G7" in `.criteria` and `.decision` != "dropped") / (count of G7-eligible reviews), and validity from those findings' `.validity` field — (b) the promotion trigger — blocking at the plan-review 0.6–0.7 band once the observation window shows an acceptable false-fire rate — and (c) where the actual promotion decision will be recorded. The actual promotion decision depends on post-deployment field data and is explicitly DEFERRED (out of this ticket's close path; it belongs with the epic's deferred measurement follow-up 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d).
- NOTE (explicit non-goal here, candidate follow-up): a material PARENT edit does not currently stale the CHILDREN'S plan-review attestations, so drift introduced by editing the epic after leaves are reviewed is only caught on the leaf's next re-review. If the observation window shows parent-edit drift, file a follow-up for parent-edit-propagating staleness in attest.compute_validity.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json (G7 routing entry), src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE; G7 grounds against the parent TICKET via the store. Per registry.py:62-65 the tool capability — filesystem + rebar `show_ticket` — is granted by the prompt's agentic `execution_mode`, i.e. AGENT_TIER membership, NOT by CODEBASE_GROUNDED); add the `require_parent_id` axis to `applies()`), src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py (`_validate_routing_entry` at 190-212 currently ignores unknown applies_at keys — add an EXPLICIT `require_parent_id` bool-type validation block so a non-boolean value raises, rather than silently passing), src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (`route_criteria()` threads the ticket's has_parent_id fact into `applies()`), src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list — criteria ARE enumerated there, so this edit is required), a new reviewers/plan_review_G7.md rubric prompt (instructs the agent to `show_ticket(parent_id)` and apply the parent-wins containment check), the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py), the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md, and tests (routing: fires on leaf-with-parent only, skipped for parentless/container/bug/session_log; semantics: fixture parent/leaf pairs — contained/exceeding/contradicting; posture pin; validate_packaged_routing()==[]; a test asserting docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md exists AND contains the fire-rate query + the literal '0.6'/'0.7' band). NO new context-assembly plumbing (G7 reads the parent via its `show_ticket` tool at runtime) and NO kernel decide.py/verify.py math changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The criterion runs ONLY on tickets that have a parent_id and no children; a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets, containers, bugs, and session_logs.
- [ ] Fixture-driven tests: a leaf narrowing its parent PASSES; a leaf adding out-of-parent-scope deliverables fires; a leaf contradicting a parent AC fires.
- [ ] The rubric and the Pass-4 coaching move state the parent-wins rule. Proving command: `grep -iE 'parent wins|show_ticket' src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G7.md` returns the parent-wins directive + the show_ticket instruction, and the new parent-realign move appears in passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY (asserted by the MOVE_REGISTRY test).
- [ ] The criterion ships advisory (0.95) with fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars. In-session proof (a COMMITTED repo file, CI-verifiable): the committed file `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` exists AND contains the promotion-decision PATH — the fire-rate/validity jq query (referencing the `plan_review_result_v1` schema and the `.criteria`/`.decision`/`.validity` fields), the literal '0.6' and '0.7' band bounds, and the recording location — proven by a unit test (tests/) that asserts the file exists AND greps it for those anchors (content check, not mere existence). The actual promotion decision is explicitly deferred (see 72b6), not required to close this ticket.
- [ ] G7's rubric instructs the AGENT to read the parent (and optionally one grandparent) via its `show_ticket` tool and compare scope — no new context-assembly plumbing is added; a test confirms G7 is registered in AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — AGENT_TIER membership is what grants the show_ticket tool (registry.py:62-65) — and the rubric references reading the parent ticket via show_ticket.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.

file_impact.reason: register G7; add require_parent_id applies_at axis
add G7 to static criteria batch list if enumerated

compiled_state.title: Plan-review criterion: leaf-plan parent-containment (leaf scope must be a subset of its parent; parent wins on conflict)
